repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Design and Environmental Analysis records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Design & Environmental Analysis

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Housing and Design

did.abstract: Oversize drawings from Professor Helen J. Cady's interior design classes in the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis at Cornell University.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: J.M. Nicholas, August 2003Daniel W. Young, March 2010

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The origins of the department of Design + Environmental Analysis and the department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design in the College of Human Ecology can be traced back to 1925, as the Department of Household Art and the Department of Textiles and Clothing. They evolved separately and then joined together in 1969 as the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis (D+EA). They stayed together until 1985, when the Department of Textiles and Apparel split from D+EA.
In November of 2021 the Department of Fiber Science and Apparel Design and the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis combined to become the Department of Human Centered Design.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIAL
relatedmaterial.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/11836

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Reports; Faculty Annual Reports Box 1; Box 2; Box 4- Folders: 15,17; Box 6- Folders: 5; Box 7- Folders: 1-51
Series II. Printed Material Box 2; Box 4- Folders: 1-6; Box 6- Folder: 5
Series III. Course Material - Syllabi Box 3- Folders: 1-40; Box 4- Folders: 6-11; Box 6- Folders: 3; Box 7- Folders: 2-8
Series IV. Department Reviews/Accreditation Box 3- Folder: 31; Box 4- Folders: 12-14; Box 7- Folder: 1
Series V. Videotapes, Slides, Films Box 6- Folders: 4
Series VI. Subject Files Box 4- Folders: 16; Box 6- Folders: 2, 6-9

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: See also College of Human Ecology records, #23-2-2817. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Design and Environmental Analysis records, #23-16-1472. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Oversize drawings from Professor Helen J. Cady's interior design classes in the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Also faculty annual reports and reports for the Department of Housing and Design (1947-1962), newsletters (1949-1958), plans (1963-1965) , course syllabi (1976-2002), flammability films (1967-1970s), and videotapes.

controlaccess.subject: Interior decoration -- Study and teaching.
Interior architecture -- Study and teaching.
Cornell University--Faculty.
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See additional faculty reports in electronic format in collection #23-2-2817

did.unittitle: DEA News-Cooperative extension publication of the DEA department
"Knit Sport Jacket"by Jean McLean and Madeline Blum
Publicity material concerning the department and faculty

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Syllabi from 2009-2015 can be found in CUL's digital repository, eCommons.

did.unittitle: Professor Helen Cady's 325 and 326 three-five week interior design course - includes lighting study unit projects by students Nancy Williams, Ruth Morgan, Zipporah Stambler and S. King
DEA 261, Fundamentals of Interior Design Studio Problems
Bound copy of DEA syllabi for the year
Proposed changes in interior design curriculum
Course 319 - The Child Oriented Environment (1973) and Course 417 - Workshop in Environmental Design (1975) - Professor Coates
Course 375 - Residential Environments - The Behavioral Basis for Design Decisions - Professor Coates
Course 219 - Design Methods: Planning Strategies - Professor Coates
Course 150 - Human and Social Factors - Professor Coates
Field study course - Designing Clothing - Professor Yackel
Poster designed by Sheila Danko's students where they found the letters of the alphabet in the built environment and nature
Banner using Cornell's charter (with Cornell 1865 in red words) designed by Rhonda Gilmore's students for Cornell's sesquicentennial
New York State 4-H clothing and textiles program: A review conducted by Gret Atkin
Foundation for interior design education research accreditation report
FIDER - Program evaluation report, required appendix materials
Correspondence regarding the termination of the MPS degree in interior design
"Building a Home Buyers Educational Program" Tape 1
"Building a Home Buyers Educational Program" Tape 2
"Building a Home Buyers Educational Program" Tape 3
"Building a Home Buyers Educational Program" slide show used for admissions
29-8mm Department flammability films, some labeled Betsy Anderson's study
Computer-aided design (CAD) labs- DEA And FSAD
Organizational chart (1997); ABC project (1997); International Workplace Studies Program -Workscape 21 (1996-1998)
Housing Education and Research Association Annual Conference "HERA's 40th anniversary:Celebrating Our Past With A Look To The Future" - Conference proceedings
